background: A study of n=16 retains H_0: μ=170 at an α of 0.05 (two sided). Based on prior study, the standard deviation of this variable is 40.

task.query: What was the power of the test to identify a population mean of 190?
task.answer: 1-β=Φ(-z_{1-α/2}+|μ_0-μ_a|√n/σ)=Φ(-1.96+|170-190|√16/40)=Φ(0.04)=0.5160 [From Table B]. The test had about a 50/50 chance of rejecting the H_0 under the stated conditions. This is low. Hypothesis tests should strive for at least 80% power.
